name: Coder Agent

personas.name: Developer persona
personas.description: Uses the execute_windows_command tool to operate VS Code entirely via GUI—menus, buttons, context-menus—placing sources under ~/Documents/CalculatorApp/src.

personas.name: Tester persona
personas.description: Uses the execute_windows_command tool to navigate VS Code GUI and create/run pytest test files under ~/Documents/CalculatorApp/tests, without typing in a shell.

personas.name: Documenter persona
personas.description: Uses the execute_windows_command tool to author and maintain documentation in ~/Documents/CalculatorApp via VS Code’s GUI.

actions: Use execute_windows_command to launch VS Code (via Start menu or desktop icon).
Use execute_windows_command to click “File → Open Folder…”, then select Documents/CalculatorApp in the file picker GUI.
Use execute_windows_command to right-click in the Explorer pane on src/, choose “New File”, and name it calculator_app.py.
Use execute_windows_command to click into the editor pane and type (or paste) the minimal CLI calculator code (+, –, *, /).
Use execute_windows_command to click the Run icon in the VS Code activity bar, select “Run Python File” in the GUI dropdown, targeting calculator_app.py.
flows.sub_goal: Implement src/calculator_app.py as a basic command-line calculator entirely through VS Code’s GUI.

actions: Use execute_windows_command to ensure VS Code is open (via GUI).
Use execute_windows_command to right-click on the tests/ folder in Explorer and choose “New File”, naming it test_calculator_app.py.
Use execute_windows_command to click into the new test file and type pytest test functions covering +, –, *, /.
Use execute_windows_command to click the Testing icon in the activity bar, locate the test_calculator_app.py test suite in the GUI sidebar, and click the green “Run” button next to it.
flows.sub_goal: Write and execute tests under tests/test_calculator_app.py via VS Code’s GUI testing interface.

actions: Use execute_windows_command to ensure VS Code is open (via GUI).
Use execute_windows_command to right-click on the workspace root in Explorer and choose “New File”, naming it README.md.
Use execute_windows_command to click into README.md and draft installation, usage examples, CLI arguments, and test instructions.
flows.sub_goal: Produce README.md documentation under ~/Documents/CalculatorApp entirely through VS Code’s GUI.

goal: Develop a minimalistic Python calculator application (`src/calculator_app.py`), complete with GUI-managed tests (`tests/test_calculator_app.py`) and documentation (`README.md`), all under `~/Documents/CalculatorApp`, using VS Code’s GUI and the `execute_windows_command` tool.
